006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
020
021/**
022 * Supported wire formats for {@link Class} values.
023 *
024 * <p>
025 * Used by {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#classFormat(ClassFormat)},
026 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Marshalled#classFormat()},
027 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledProp#classFormat()}, and
028 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledConfig#classFormat()} to control how {@link Class} values
029 * are written to text-based wire formats.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * The default is {@link #FQCN} which mirrors the historical {@code ClassSwap} behavior (canonical dotted
033 * name with sensible fallback to the JVM binary name).
034 *
035 * <h5 class='topic'>FQCN vs BINARY_NAME for nested classes</h5>
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * For nested-type encoding the two forms diverge:
039 * <ul>
040 *    <li>{@link #FQCN} uses {@link Class#getCanonicalName()} when available — {@code "java.util.Map.Entry"}
041 *       — and falls back to {@link Class#getName()} for local / anonymous classes (where
042 *       {@code getCanonicalName()} returns {@code null}).
043 *    <li>{@link #BINARY_NAME} always uses {@link Class#getName()} — {@code "java.util.Map$Entry"}.
044 * </ul>
045 *
046 * <p>
047 * For arrays the difference is more dramatic:
048 * <ul>
049 *    <li>{@link #FQCN} produces {@code "int[]"} / {@code "java.lang.String[]"}.
050 *    <li>{@link #BINARY_NAME} produces {@code "[I"} / {@code "[Ljava.lang.String;"} (JVM descriptor form).
051 * </ul>
052 *
053 * <h5 class='topic'>SIMPLE_NAME is serialize-only</h5>
054 *
055 * <p>
056 * {@link #SIMPLE_NAME} discards package and outer-class context and is <b>not round-trip safe</b>. The
057 * parser path for this constant throws {@link UnsupportedOperationException}; use {@link #FQCN} or
058 * {@link #BINARY_NAME} for round-trippable wires.
059 *
060 * <h5 class='topic'>Precedence (highest to lowest)</h5>
061 * <ol>
062 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledProp#classFormat() @MarshalledProp(classFormat=…)} on the bean property.
063 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Marshalled#classFormat() @Marshalled(classFormat=…)} on the bean class.
064 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledConfig#classFormat() @MarshalledConfig(classFormat=…)} on
065 *       <code><ja>@Rest</ja></code>-annotated classes / methods.
066 *    <li>Programmatic {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#classFormat(ClassFormat)}.
067 *    <li>Environment variable <c>MarshallingContext.classFormat</c>.
068 *    <li>The default constant ({@link #FQCN}).
069 * </ol>
070 *
071 * <h5 class='topic'>Parser leniency</h5>
072 *
073 * <p>
074 * Parsers SHALL try {@link Class#forName(String, boolean, ClassLoader)} first regardless of the
075 * parser-side {@code ClassFormat} setting — this handles {@link #BINARY_NAME} and most {@link #FQCN}
076 * inputs unambiguously. For nested-type {@link #FQCN} input ({@code "java.util.Map.Entry"}), the parser
077 * walks back through dotted segments from right to left, replacing each uppercase-prefixed dot with
078 * {@code $} and retrying — this resolves multi-level nesting like
079 * {@code "com.example.Outer.Inner.Deepest"} to {@code com.example.Outer$Inner$Deepest}.  For
080 * {@link #FQCN} array input the parser strips trailing {@code []} pairs, resolves the leaf component
081 * type (including primitives like {@code "int"}), and rebuilds the array via {@link Class#arrayType()}
082 * — so {@code "int[]"} resolves to {@code int[].class} and {@code "java.lang.String[][]"} resolves to
083 * {@code String[][].class}.
084 *
085 * <h5 class='topic'>Binary serializers</h5>
086 *
087 * <p>
088 * Binary serializers (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack / Prototext / Parquet) emit a UTF-8 string regardless of this
089 * setting — there is no native {@code Class} wire type in any supported binary format.
090 */
091public enum ClassFormat {
092
093   /** Sentinel meaning "no value configured" — falls through to the next-higher precedence level. */
094   NOT_SET,
095
096   /**
097    * Canonical fully-qualified name with dotted nested-type separators (the default).
098    *
099    * <p>
100    * Uses {@link Class#getCanonicalName()} when available; falls back to {@link Class#getName()} for
101    * local / anonymous classes where {@code getCanonicalName()} returns {@code null}.
102    *
103    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
104    * <p class='bcode'>
105    *    java.lang.String
106    *    java.util.Map.Entry
107    *    int[]
108    * </p>
109    */
110   FQCN,
111
112   /**
113    * JVM binary name per {@link Class#getName()}.
114    *
115    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
116    * <p class='bcode'>
117    *    java.lang.String
118    *    java.util.Map$Entry
119    *    [Ljava.lang.String;
120    *    [I
121    * </p>
122    *
123    * <p>
124    * Identical to {@link #FQCN} for top-level non-array reference types; diverges for nested types
125    * ({@code $} vs {@code .}) and arrays (JVM descriptor vs {@code Type[]} form).
126    */
127   BINARY_NAME,
128
129   /**
130    * Unqualified simple name per {@link Class#getSimpleName()}.
131    *
132    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
133    * <p class='bcode'>
134    *    String
135    *    Entry
136    *    int
137    * </p>
138    *
139    * <p>
140    * <b>Serialize-only.</b> The parser path for this constant throws
141    * {@link UnsupportedOperationException} — there is no defensible way to resolve {@code "Map"} back
142    * to a unique {@link Class} without a registry hint, and adding such a hint is out of scope.
143    */
144   SIMPLE_NAME;
145
146   /**
147    * Formats the specified {@link Class} using this format.
148    *
149    * <p>
150    * {@link #NOT_SET} / {@code null} format are treated as {@link #FQCN}.
151    *
152    * @param value The value to format. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
153    * @param format The configured format. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link #FQCN}).
154    * @return The formatted wire representation, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
155    */
156   public static String format(Class<?> value, ClassFormat format) {
157      if (value == null)
158         return null;
159      var fmt = format == null ? FQCN : format;
160      return switch (fmt) {
161         case NOT_SET, FQCN -> {
162            // getCanonicalName() returns null for local / anonymous classes; fall back to binary name.
163            var canonical = value.getCanonicalName();
164            yield canonical != null ? canonical : value.getName();
165         }
166         case BINARY_NAME -> value.getName();
167         case SIMPLE_NAME -> value.getSimpleName();
168      };
169   }
170
171   /**
172    * Parses the specified wire value into a {@link Class}.
173    *
174    * <p>
175    * Lenient parsing — handles every shape produced by {@link #format(Class, ClassFormat)} for the
176    * round-trippable formats ({@link #FQCN} and {@link #BINARY_NAME}), and accepts cross-form input
177    * as well (an {@link #FQCN} parser still resolves {@link #BINARY_NAME} input and vice versa).
178    *
179    * <p>
180    * Resolution steps:
181    * <ol>
182    *    <li>Strip trailing {@code []} pairs ({@link #FQCN} array form), tracking dimension count.  Empty
183    *       brackets at the leading edge (JVM descriptor form like {@code "[I"} /
184    *       {@code "[Ljava.lang.String;"}) are left to {@link Class#forName(String, boolean, ClassLoader)}
185    *       because that method already handles them.
186    *    <li>Resolve the leaf component:
187    *       <ul>
188    *          <li>If the leaf names a primitive type ({@code "int"}, {@code "boolean"}, etc. — emitted
189    *             by {@link #FQCN} for primitive arrays), return the corresponding {@code TYPE} class.
190    *          <li>Otherwise try {@link Class#forName(String, boolean, ClassLoader)} directly.
191    *          <li>On failure, walk dots from right to left, replacing each uppercase-prefixed dot with
192    *             {@code $} (cumulatively — already-replaced dots stay replaced) and retrying.  This
193    *             resolves multi-level nesting like {@code "com.example.Outer.Inner.Deepest"} to
194    *             {@code com.example.Outer$Inner$Deepest}.  Dots whose trailing segment starts with a
195    *             lowercase letter are treated as package boundaries and skipped.
196    *       </ul>
197    *    <li>Apply {@link Class#arrayType()} once per stripped dimension to rebuild the array class.
198    * </ol>
199    *
200    * <p>
201    * {@link #SIMPLE_NAME} is not supported on the parser side — throws
202    * {@link UnsupportedOperationException}.
203    *
204    * @param value The wire value. Can be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
205    * @param format The configured format hint. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link #FQCN}).
206    * @param classLoader The class loader. Can be <jk>null</jk> (uses
207    *   {@link Thread#getContextClassLoader() context class loader}).
208    * @return The parsed {@link Class}, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
209    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the value cannot be resolved.
210    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If {@code format} is {@link #SIMPLE_NAME}.
211    */
212   public static Class<?> parse(String value, ClassFormat format, ClassLoader classLoader) {
213      if (value == null)
214         return null;
215      var s = value.trim();
216      if (s.isEmpty())
217         return null;
218      var fmt = format == null ? FQCN : format;
219      if (fmt == SIMPLE_NAME)
220         throw new UnsupportedOperationException("SIMPLE_NAME is serialize-only — use FQCN or BINARY_NAME for round-trippable wires");
221      var cl = classLoader != null ? classLoader : Thread.currentThread().getContextClassLoader();
222      try {
223         return resolveWithArraySuffix(s, cl);
224      } catch (ClassNotFoundException firstAttempt) {
225         throw illegalArg("Could not resolve class ''{0}'' for format {1}: {2}", value, format, firstAttempt.getMessage());
226      }
227   }
228
229   /**
230    * Strips trailing {@code []} pairs from the FQCN array form, resolves the leaf component, then
231    * rebuilds the array dimensions via {@link Class#arrayType()}.
232    *
233    * <p>
234    * Inputs that don't end in {@code []} (top-level classes and JVM-descriptor array forms like
235    * {@code "[I"}) pass straight through to {@link #resolveLeaf(String, ClassLoader)}.
236    */
237   private static Class<?> resolveWithArraySuffix(String s, ClassLoader cl) throws ClassNotFoundException {
238      var dims = 0;
239      var leaf = s;
240      while (leaf.endsWith("[]")) {
241         dims++;
242         leaf = leaf.substring(0, leaf.length() - 2);
243      }
244      if (dims == 0)
245         return resolveLeaf(s, cl);
246      if (leaf.isEmpty())
247         throw new ClassNotFoundException(s);
248      var result = resolveLeaf(leaf, cl);
249      for (var i = 0; i < dims; i++)
250         result = result.arrayType();
251      return result;
252   }
253
254   /**
255    * Resolves a non-array leaf token to a {@link Class}.
256    *
257    * <p>
258    * Checks the primitive-name table first ({@link #FQCN} emits {@code "int"} / {@code "boolean"} /
259    * etc. for primitive array leafs), then falls through to {@link Class#forName(String, boolean,
260    * ClassLoader)} with a multi-level nested-type fallback heuristic.
261    */
262   private static Class<?> resolveLeaf(String s, ClassLoader cl) throws ClassNotFoundException {
263      var prim = primitiveByName(s);
264      if (prim != null)
265         return prim;
266      try {
267         return Class.forName(s, true, cl);
268      } catch (ClassNotFoundException firstAttempt) {
269         var nested = nestedTypeFallback(s, cl);
270         if (nested != null)
271            return nested;
272         throw firstAttempt;
273      }
274   }
275
276   /**
277    * Walks dots from right to left, replacing each uppercase-prefixed dot with {@code $} and retrying
278    * {@link Class#forName(String, boolean, ClassLoader)}.  Replacements accumulate — once a dot has
279    * been replaced, it stays replaced for subsequent iterations — so multi-level nesting like
280    * {@code "Outer.Inner.Deepest"} resolves to {@code "Outer$Inner$Deepest"}.
281    *
282    * <p>
283    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if no rewrite resolves to a loadable class.  Dots whose trailing segment
284    * starts with a lowercase letter are treated as package boundaries and left alone.
285    */
286   private static Class<?> nestedTypeFallback(String s, ClassLoader cl) {
287      var chars = s.toCharArray();
288      for (var i = chars.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
289         if (chars[i] == '.' && i + 1 < chars.length && Character.isUpperCase(chars[i + 1])) {
290            chars[i] = '$';
291            try {
292               return Class.forName(new String(chars), true, cl);
293            } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") ClassNotFoundException ignored) {
294               // Keep walking; the previous-dot replacement persists in chars so deeper nesting can resolve.
295            }
296         }
297      }
298      return null;
299   }
300
301   private static Class<?> primitiveByName(String s) {
302      return switch (s) {
303         case "boolean" -> boolean.class;
304         case "byte" -> byte.class;
305         case "char" -> char.class;
306         case "short" -> short.class;
307         case "int" -> int.class;
308         case "long" -> long.class;
309         case "float" -> float.class;
310         case "double" -> double.class;
311         case "void" -> void.class;
312         default -> null;
313      };
314   }
315
316   /**
317    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this format emits a numeric wire value.
318    *
319    * <p>
320    * Always <jk>false</jk> — every constant emits a textual representation.
321    *
322    * @return <jk>false</jk>.
323    */
324   @SuppressWarnings({
325      "static-method", // Kept as an instance method for polymorphic-by-convention symmetry with the other Format classes (BigNumberFormat, FloatFormat, DurationFormat, etc.) where isNumeric() depends on the enum constant.
326      "java:S3400"     // Same rationale — must remain an instance method, not a constant, to match the cross-Format API contract.
327   })
328   public boolean isNumeric() {
329      return false;
330   }
331}
